text.title: Agentic RAG Tool-Using — Procedure
text.summary: 6-step procedure to wire a tool-using RAG agent: define registry → write tool descriptions → run capped loop → cache → synthesise → log trace.

section.title: Step 1 — Define tool registry
section.p: Build a `dict[str, Callable[[str], list[dict]]]` mapping tool name to callable. Minimum useful registry: vector_search, keyword_search, sql_query, web_search, generate_answer (sentinel). Each callable returns a list of `{id, text, score, source}` dicts.

section.title: Step 2 — Write specific tool descriptions
section.p: For each tool, write a one-line description naming verb + input + result shape. Pass these to the LLM verbatim as the `tools` block. Apply rule r2-specific-descriptions.

section.title: Step 3 — Run the capped loop
section.p: Loop `for _ in range(max_calls)`: ask the routing model which tool to invoke next given (query, accumulated context summary). Execute selected tool. Append `{iteration, tool, query, result_summary[:200], latency_ms}` to the trace. Break when selector returns `generate_answer`. Enforce r1-cap-and-log.

section.title: Step 4 — Cache intra-run
section.p: Key cache by `(tool_name, normalised_query)`. Before invoking, check cache; on hit, record `cached: true` in the trace and skip the network call. Clear the cache when the agent run completes. Apply r4-intra-run-cache.

section.title: Step 5 — Synthesise the final answer
section.p: Hand the full accumulated context plus the original user question to the heavier synthesis model. Constrain the system prompt: "answer only from the supplied context; if information is missing say so explicitly". Cite each claim with the trace entry that produced it.

section.title: Step 6 — Emit the JSON trace
section.p: Return the object specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`. Run `scripts/validate-agentic-rag-tool-using.py` on the emitted object before passing the answer to downstream consumers.
